questions.question: What is Statistics?
options: The study of collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data
The study of shapes and angles
The study of numbers only
The study of equations

questions.question: What is a data set?
options: A collection of data values
A single number
A mathematical equation
A geometric shape

questions.question: What is the mean of a data set?
options: The sum of all values divided by the number of values
The middle value when arranged in order
The most frequently occurring value
The difference between the largest and smallest values

questions.question: What is the median of a data set?
options: The sum of all values divided by the number of values
The middle value when arranged in order
The most frequently occurring value
The difference between the largest and smallest values

questions.question: What is the mode of a data set?
options: The sum of all values divided by the number of values
The middle value when arranged in order
The most frequently occurring value
The difference between the largest and smallest values

questions.question: What is the range of a data set?
options: The sum of all values divided by the number of values
The middle value when arranged in order
The most frequently occurring value
The difference between the largest and smallest values

questions.question: What is the mean of 2, 4, and 6?
What is the mean of 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5?
What is the median of 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5?
What is the median of 2, 4, 6, and 8?
What is the mode of 1, 2, 2, 3, and 4?
What is the mode of 3, 3, 4, 5, and 5?
options: Both 3 and 5

questions.question: What is the range of 1, 3, 5, and 7?
What is the range of 2, 8, 10, and 15?
What is probability?
options: The likelihood of an event occurring
The total number of outcomes
The sum of all events
The average of outcomes

questions.question: What is the formula for probability?
options: Number of favorable outcomes / Total number of outcomes
Total number of outcomes / Number of favorable outcomes
Number of favorable outcomes × Total number of outcomes
Number of favorable outcomes - Total number of outcomes

questions.question: What is the probability of getting heads when tossing a fair coin?
What is the probability of getting tails when tossing a fair coin?
What is the probability of rolling a 3 on a fair die?
What is the probability of rolling an even number on a fair die?
What is a random experiment?
options: An experiment where the outcome is uncertain
An experiment where the outcome is certain
An experiment with no outcomes
An experiment with one outcome

questions.question: What is a sample space?
options: The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment
The set of favorable outcomes
The set of unlikely outcomes
The set of impossible outcomes

questions.question: What is an event in probability?
options: A set of outcomes of an experiment
The total number of outcomes
The impossible outcome
The certain outcome

questions.question: What is a certain event?
options: An event that will definitely happen
An event that will never happen
An event that may happen
An event that is unlikely

questions.question: What is an impossible event?
options: An event that will definitely happen
An event that will never happen
An event that may happen
An event that is likely

questions.question: What is the probability of a certain event?
options: Undefined

questions.question: What is the probability of an impossible event?
options: Undefined

questions.question: What is a frequency distribution?
options: A table that shows how often each value occurs
A table that shows the mean of data
A table that shows the median of data
A table that shows the range of data

questions.question: What is a cumulative frequency?
options: The sum of frequencies up to a certain point
The product of frequencies
The average of frequencies
The difference of frequencies

questions.question: What is a histogram?
options: A bar graph that shows frequency distribution
A pie chart
A line graph
A scatter plot

questions.question: What is a bar chart?
options: A graph that uses rectangular bars to represent data
A graph that uses lines to represent data
A graph that uses circles to represent data
A graph that uses dots to represent data

questions.question: What is a pie chart?
options: A circular graph divided into slices
A graph with rectangular bars
A graph with lines
A graph with dots

questions.question: What is the mean of 5, 10, and 15?
What is the median of 3, 7, 9, 12, and 15?
What is the mode of 2, 4, 4, 6, and 8?
What is the range of 3, 7, 11, and 15?
What is the probability of drawing a red card from a standard deck of 52 cards?
What is the probability of drawing a king from a standard deck of 52 cards?
What is the probability of drawing a heart from a standard deck of 52 cards?
What is the sum of all probabilities in a sample space?
options: Undefined
